summary: Jan Schaumann, Chief Information Security Architect at Akamai, emphasizes that quantum threats extend beyond the Transport Layer Security protocol to a wide array of networked applications using vulnerable asymmetric cryptography, such as Secure Shell, email, and messaging systems. Akamai is rolling out post-quantum cryptography support with hybrid key exchange algorithms (notably X25519MLKEM768) across its Enhanced Transport Layer Security services by default. Notably, OpenSSH adopted support for Streamlined NTRU Prime in 2021, and added FIPS 203 Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism hybrids in 2024, with post-quantum algorithms now default in version 9.9 and active warnings against unsafe key exchanges in version 10.1 and above. This highlights the increasing integration of quantum-safe cryptography in widely used protocols and tools in 2024.
